S49: Diskusjon Vi har oppsummert data fra en systematisk oversikt og flere randomiserte kontrol- lerte og prospektive kontrollerte studier for å undersøke sykdomsforløp, funksjon i dagliglivet og bivirkninger hos personer som bruker lavdose naltrekson, men uten å kunne konkludere om det er effektivt og sikkert.

S68: 6 Key messages (English) Key messages (English) In Norway, naltrexone is approved as supportive treatment of alcohol dependence.
S69: The recommended dose is 50 mg, equivalent to the mar- keted tablet.
S70: Naltrexone in much lower doses than 50 mg has been used in Norway for the treatment of a variety of diseases, such as mul- tiple sclerosis (MS), Crohn's disease, fibromyalgia, cancer, inflamma- tory bowel disease, chronic fatigue syndrome, and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
S71: Doses of 3 to 5 mg per day have often been termed low dose naltrexone.
S72: This use is beyond the approved indication.
S73: The purpose of this report is to examine whether there is a documented effect of the use of naltrexone in low doses.
S74: We summarized data from a systematic review and several random- ized controlled and prospective controlled studies in order to investi- gate the effect of using naltrexone in low doses on illness, and on func- tioning in daily life and to examine the risk of side effects.
S75: We identified studies for people with: • Crohn's disease (one systematic review, two studies) • multiple sclerosis (two studies) • fibromyalgia (two studies) • cancer (one study) • HIV (one study) • various pain conditions (three studies) • opioid dependence (six studies) All studies were either small, of short duration, or had other methodo- logical limitations.
S76: We considered the documentation to have very low quality.
S77: That means that we can not conclude whether the use of nal- trexone in low doses is effective or safe.

S81: ------------------------------------------ Peer review: Lillebeth Larun, researcher, The Norwegian Knowledge Centre for the Health Service (NOKC) Signe Flottorp, researcher, NOKC Ståle Onsgård Sagabråten, General Practitioner, Nesbyen legesenter Kjell-Morten Myhr, Professor/Consultant neurologist, University of Bergen and Haukeland University Hospital 7 Executive summary (English) Executive summary (English) Background In Norway, naltrexone (Naltrexone POA Pharma) is approved as supportive treat- ment of alcohol dependence.
S82: The recommended dose is 50 mg, equivalent to the marketed tablet.
S83: Naltrexone in much lower doses than 50 mg has been used in Nor- way for the treatment of a variety of diseases, such as multiple sclerosis (MS), Crohn's disease, fibromyalgia, cancer, inflammatory bowel disease, chronic fatigue syndrome, and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
S84: Doses of 3 to 5 mg per day have often been termed low dose naltrexone.
S85: This use is beyond the approved indication.
S86: The purpose of this report is to examine whether there is a documented effect of the use of naltrexone in low doses.
S87: Objective Our objective was to examine efficacy and safety of any use of naltrexone in doses of 5 mg/day or lower.
S88: Method We searched systematically for systematic reviews and randomized controlled or prospective controlled trials on the efficacy and safety of naltrexone in low doses.
S89: Two researcher working independently screened all titles and abstracts according to inclusion and exclusion criteria.
S90: The quality of the included publications was as- sessed in the same way.
S91: We summarized the results in text and tables and used GRADE to establish our confidence in the effect estimates.
S92: Results We identified studies that met our inclusion criteria for people with Crohn's disease, multiple sclerosis, fibromyalgia, cancer, HIV, various pain disorders, and opioid de- pendence.
S93: 8 Executive summary (English) We included a systematic review on patients with inflammatory bowel disease, two studies on patients with MS, two studies on patients with fibromyalgia, a study on patients with cancer, a study on patients with HIV, three studies of patients with pain conditions and six studies of patients with opioid dependence.
S94: All studies were either small, of short duration or had other methodological limita- tions.
S95: We considered the documentation to have very low quality.
S96: Studies had un- clear or high risk of bias, the results had unclear reproducibility, few events, wide confidence intervals or distribution of the results was not provided.
S97: That means that we can not conclude whether the use of naltrexone in low doses is effective or safe.
S98: Discussion We summarized data from a systematic review and several randomized controlled and prospective controlled studies in order to investigate the effect of using naltrex- one in low doses on illness and, functioning in daily life, and to examine the risk of side effects.
S99: We were not able to conclude whether such use is effective and safe.
S100: The studies that included people with Crohn’s disease, fibromyalgia, multiple sclero- sis, cancer and HIV used naltrexone in daily doses of between 3 and 5 mg.
S101: The treat- ment time was usually between 8 and 12 weeks.
S102: These studies describe themselves as pilot studies, the first controlled study, or similar, and are accordingly of short duration.
S103: However, the choice of treatment duration is important.
S104: It must be long enough to allow a change to occur and be measured.
S105: The studies in question are not able to answer questions about the efficacy and safety of long term use.
S106: The studies that examined naltrexone in low doses for patients with chronic pain and opioid dependence had shorter treatment duration and / or lower doses than in studies related to other conditions.
S107: Doses ranged from 0.0001 mg to 1 mg. We did not investigate potential dose-findings studies, so we have no evidence to estimate the dose interval where naltrexone could have an effect.
S108: It may therefore be that the doses used creates uncertainty about whether not an effect could be expected.
S109: The included studies investigated several outcomes that could potentially be meas- ured after a short time, such as daily pain, "craving for substance" or withdrawal symptoms.
S110: Because of the short treatment duration, the studies could not give an- swers relating to efficacy and safety of long term use.
S111: The included studies were generally small.
S112: It is unlikely that the studies included in this section could have shown a difference between treatment groups with certainty, even if there was a difference.
S113: Moreover, it is extremely uncertain whether low-dose naltrexone has been investigated in a sufficient number of patients to identify rare side effects, and to determine whether there may be relative differences in the fre- quency of rare adverse events between different treatment options.
S114: 9 Executive summary (English) Conclusion Based on current the evidence, it is not possible to determine whether low-dose nal- trexone is effective and safe.
S115: Neither is it possible to determine whether there are differences in efficacy between different patient groups.
S116: We identified studies that included people with Crohn's disease, multiple sclerosis, fibromyalgia, cancer, HIV, addiction problems and various chronic pain disorders and opioid dependence.
S117: The studies were small, of short duration, had uncertain es- timates for effect and safety and other methodological limitations that overall made it impossible to draw any conclusions.
S118: There is a need for well-planned and well-conducted RCTs of long enough duration to reliably capture any effects, to investigate whether effects persists over time, and if side effects occur during long-term use.

S555: Abstinenssymptomer etter seponering av buprenorfin Kosten og medarbeidere (19) inkluderte pasienter hvor buprenorfin ble seponert, og de undersøkte om det var forskjell i abstinenssymptomer avhengig av om pasienten fikk placebo, nalokson eller naltrekson.
S556: Studien gikk over fire dager, på dag to ble buprenorfin seponert, på dag tre fikk alle placebo og på dag fire fikk deltakerne 1 mg naltrekson eller 0,5 mg/kg nalokson.
S557: Symptomer ble målt ved hjelp av 15 kriterier, på en skala fra 0-3 (totalscore 0-45) som inkluderte selvrapporterte kriterier som «sug etter stoff», anoreksi, muskelsmerter, nedsatt energi samt observasjoner gjort av blindet sykepleier: irritabilitet, rastløshet, gjesping, gåsehud, skjelving, svetting, kvalme, oppkast, diaré, rennende øyne og rennende nese.

S664: 47 Konklusjon Konklusjon Vi søkte etter dokumentasjon for alle typer pasienter som benyttet lavdose naltrek- son og var åpne for ulike sammenlikningsalternativ, som ingen behandling, placebo eller standard behandling for sykdommen.
S665: Vi identifiserte studier for personer med Crohns sykdom, multippel sklerose, fibromyalgi, kreft, hiv, opioidavhengighet og ulike smertetilstander.
